notes: Launch-ready Grade 8 card with reading, vocabulary, journals, writing, questions, informational text, and assessments.
Structured from existing markdown unit card.

book.title: SHERLOCK HOLMES UNIT CARD
book.description: 8th Grade Days 61-75 (Weeks 13-15) --- Unit Overview Stories: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle (adapted) Genre: Detective Mystery / Logical Deduction Theme: Observation, Logic, Scientific Method, Justice, Intellect vs. Crime Total Chapters: 24 Days: 15 (Mon-Fri for 3 weeks) Assessment Day: Day 15 (Friday, Week 15)

weeks.title: Week 28: SHERLOCK HOLMES UNIT CARD Part 1 (Days 136-140)
vocabulary: Deduction: define, use in context, and connect to the text
literary evidence: embed and explain a quotation
academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Deduction? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write an analytical paragraph about setting, conflict, or character introduction.
Strengthen one response by adding a precise quotation and commentary.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Review day: discussion, revision, and evidence check.

weeks.title: Week 29: SHERLOCK HOLMES UNIT CARD Part 2 (Days 141-145)
vocabulary: Justice: define, use in context, and connect to the text

academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Justice? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write a comparison paragraph connecting the fiction to the paired informational text.
Strengthen one response by adding a precise quotation and commentary.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Review day: discussion, revision, and evidence check.

weeks.title: Week 30: SHERLOCK HOLMES UNIT CARD Part 3 (Days 146-150)
vocabulary: Observation: define, use in context, and connect to the text
literary evidence: embed and explain a quotation
academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Observation? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write a short literary analysis paragraph about theme, author craft, or moral complexity.
Revise one paragraph into the end-of-card portfolio response.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Book-card assessment: passage analysis, vocabulary, and literary analysis paragraph.
